feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Extended Warehouse Management
feed.subtitle: SAP Extended Warehouse Management Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Public cloud WM - Outbound pick task is not picking the destination BIN S920
entry.content:
Hello team,

Could you please tell me which config i am missing from starter system , simple ask over here. During picking system is not finding staging storage type as well picking bin

entry.title: Error "/SCWM/L3690: Enter a destination HU" during RF Putaway WT Confirmation
entry.content:
We are encountering a discrepancy in RF Putaway behavior between the standard Starter Warehouse and a custom Warehouse within the same Starter System.

1. Symptom When performing RF Warehouse Order (WO) Putaway to a storage bin in Warehouse (Storage Type S001), which was provided by the Starter System, the following error occurs:

  • Error Message: Enter a destination HU (Message ID: /SCWM/L3690)

2. Comparison In the same Starter System environment, we created a new custom Warehouse (Storage Type ZRM01). When performing RF Putaway in this warehouse, the error does not occur, and the process is completed successfully without a destination HU.

There is no difference in the settings between the two storage types. I am curious which specific configuration in the standard warehouse is triggering this error.

entry.title: The function module CONVERT_BDCMSGCOLL_TO_BAPIRET2 does not exist in the system S/4 release 2023
entry.content:
Hi

We have upgraded to HANA 2023 and we had used the function module CONVERT_BDCMSGCOLL_TO_BAPIRET2 in some of our programs.  As per SAP note 3411865, The function module CONVERT_BDCMSGCOLL_TO_BAPIRET2 does not exist in the system S/4 release 2023. We have checked for any replacement FM but didn't find any. This note is also not implementable. Kindly provide an alternate FM as we need this functionality for some of our developments

 

Regards

Vijay

entry.title: Destination bin requires handling unit /scwm/rf_en223
entry.content:
Hola Gurú

Tengo un problema, a continuación se detallan los siguientes puntos:

Al recoger el material con su unidad de manipulación, para su reposición se muestra el siguiente error:

El contenedor de destino requiere la unidad de manipulación  scwm rf_en223

¿Podrías ayudarme, por favor?

Saludos

Marcelo

entry.title: WT confirmation via /SCWM/RFUI does not trigger Suitable enhancement / BADI after posting
entry.content:
The ask is to trigger a web service automatically when a Warehouse Task is confirmed and posted to the database for a particular warehouse num (LGNUM).

Analysis Performed:Debugged RFUI WT confirmation flow Traced execution through function modules and classes Verified that: No relevant BAdI (CALL BADI) is triggered during RF confirmation.

 Checked potential function module: /SCWM/TO_CONFIRM Identified as a possible common posting point, but not confirmed as enhancement-safeQuestion to SAP:

  1. What is the recommended enhancement point (BAdI/User Exit) to trigger custom logic after WT confirmation that works for both: /SCWM/TO_CONF and /SCWM/RFUI 
  2. Is there any standard BAdI or enhancement spot available at the common posting layer of WT confirmation?
  3. Can /SCWM/TO_CONFIRM be safely enhanced for this purpose, or is there a standard alternative?

entry.title: Batch number creation should not dictate EWM distribution
entry.content:
Hello team,

 

I have created two ASN.

180000050 – Batch number I did not assign during ASN creation

180000049 – Batch number I have assigned during ASN creation

 

But Batch number creation should not dictate which IBD will be distributed or which IBD should not be disturbed in WM public cloud

entry.title: Automatic picking of consignement stock
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working on an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud implementation with WM, and I would like to confirm the standard behavior regarding automatic picking from consignment stock.

Context

  • Outbound process with automatic warehouse task creation from an outbound delivery
  • Product available in a single storage bin
  • The bin contains:
    • Own stock (non‑consignment)
    • Consignment stock
  • Both stock types are visible at bin level and can be picked manually without any issue

Observed behavior

  • During automatic warehouse task creation, the system:
    • Picks the own stock first from the bin
    • Once the own stock is depleted, the bin is ignored, even though consignment stock is still physically available
    • The system then searches another bin with own stock, or no WT is created
  • However, during manual picking, the user can explicitly select and pick the consignment stock from the same bin

Question

Is this behavior standard by design in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud WM?

More specifically:

  • Is consignment stock intentionally excluded from automatic stock removal / automatic WT creation?
  • Is automatic picking from consignment stock not supported unless a manual selection or a prior posting change (consignment → own stock) is performed?
  • Are there any standard configuration options in Public Cloud WM to allow automatic picking of consignment stock, or is this intentionally restricted to manual processes only?

The behavior looks consistent and logical from a valuation and control perspective, but I would like to confirm whether this is:

  • a standard limitation,
  • or if there is an official recommendation / design decision documented by SAP.

Thank you in advance for your insights.

entry.title: Picking sequence: Pick, pack, pass
entry.content:
Hi SAP EWM Community,

we use Pick, Pack, Pass in the warehouse and essentially have three activity areas:

Activity Area A
Activity Area B
Activity Area C

They are joined under 1 Higher Level Activity Area ABC. 

For production supply, the optimal picking sequence is A → B → C as Production is next to C. For distribution, our optimal sequence would be C → B → A as Loading Area is next to A. 

We haven’t yet found a way to change the picking sequence based on the process, e.g., warehouse process type.

Is this possible within SAP Standard ? Does anyone have an idea?

Thank you in advance,
Nicolas

entry.title: How to dynamically determine Warehouse Source Storage Type by Customer for the same material code
entry.content:
Hi

A single material code is being sold to multiple customers (A, B, C).

We want to store and pick this material from separate Storage Types assigned to each customer (e.g., ST_A, ST_B, ST_C).

However, only one Stock Removal Control Indicator can be set in the Material Master.

In this case, is there a way to dynamically determine the source storage type and bin when a Warehouse Order is created based on an Outbound Delivery?

For example, can we ensure that the source storage type and bin are determined as "ST_A" for Customer A, and "ST_B" for Customer B?

entry.title: PMR staging
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

While creating staging warehouse task in -/SCWM/STAGE

There are 5 components in production order that needs to be stage

Out of 5 components ,4 Components is having stock and for one component stock is not present.

When I’m trying to create stating warehouse task in /SCWM/STAGE with reference to production order system is not creating WT for components with available qty

So businesses expectation is that it should create WT for components with available stock qty

 

Thanks,

Virsinh Jadhav

entry.title: WT Creation Issue in S/4HANA Public Cloud EWM – Foreground vs Background Behavior
entry.content:
Guys, I am facing an issue related to WT creation in S/4HANA Public Cloud EWM.

What I did was: I created a custom storage type, defined a Putaway Control Indicator (PCI), and maintained the Storage Type Search Sequence (STSS). I also assigned the STSS to the storage type and mapped the PCI with the STSS using the configuration node “Specify STSS in SIMG.”

Then, I assigned the PCI to the product master and posted a GR. When I tried to create a WT for putaway, the system created the WT with a standard bin for putaway (S970 – clarification zone bins).

However, when I created the WT using the “Change Inbound Delivery – Deliveries” app, the system created the WT for my custom bin.

In simple terms, foreground WT creation (via Fiori app) is working as expected, but background WT creation (via Warehouse Monitor) is not.

The same issue occurs for outbound as well. I created an activity area and assigned it to the storage type, defined a stock removal indicator and assigned it to the product master, and also maintained the storage type search sequence with the removal indicator.

This setup works in the “Manage Outbound Delivery Orders” app, but not in the Warehouse Monitor, where it shows the bin determination error mentioned below.

I hope I made my problem statement clear. Waiting for your responses

entry.title: Changing the shipping point after integration with TM
entry.content:
Hello everybody, Ilia is here.

I'm looking for solution advice for a given business process and system landscape. I would be grateful to hear your comments:

Process:

We are currently in an OrderToCash process involving the following SAP modules: SD, LE, TM and EWM.
High level business process looks like this:
At regular intervals, the customer submits a request for the delivery of specific materials by a specific date. The request is processed outside SAP, but information regarding when and in what quantity the customer expects delivery is received in SAP via an interface.
Next, we need to locate the material in the warehouse, prepare it for transport and load it onto the scheduled truck.
Logistically, the process ends once the vehicle has left the company premises. Subsequent financial processes have been omitted for the sake of simplicity.

There is a logistical feature to the process:

Sometimes large quantities of materials are produced, and any surplus is then transported to an external warehouse.

When preparing the transport planning, the planner assesses the stock levels of materials at the on-site warehouse and at the external warehouse. If there are sufficient quantities of materials at the external warehouse to fulfil the delivery, it will be dispatched from there.

 System reflection of the process:

We are working on a brownfield implementation with existing sales and planning processes that cannot be changed on the fly. We are in ASR context.

Contracts have been concluded with customers, and the aforementioned recurring delivery requests are recorded in sales via Sales Agreements. When a customer requests a new delivery, a new schedule line is created in the Sales Agreement.


This part of the process already exists and cannot be changed at present.

Another point: each sales agreement also implies the delivery of only one material. By default, a schedule line in a Sales Agreement is assigned to a shipping point. In our case, this is the shipping point of our factory rather than an external warehouse.
Consequently, after a schedule line is created, it is integrated into the Transport Management (TM) module, where the freight unit inherits the shipping point from the schedule line.
An outbound delivery will be created from the TM module, incorporating the material quantity, shipping point, and consignee. 
The challenge we faced: we would like to be able to change the shipping point during transport planning to dispatch goods from a different origin location. -> an external warehouse in our example. Ideally, using standard functions, but personally, I have little faith that this is possible.

What we have already tried and what can be excluded:

1. Creating deliveries from LE and changing the shipping point there, before distribution to TM. Copy control between SA and Delivery does not allow this.
2. Changing the Sales Agreement via the transport planner is not permitted by organisational policies.
3. Creating individual sales orders with a specified shipping point is also not permitted for organisational reasons.

We will most likely have to develop a custom solution, but I decided to seek advice to see if there might be a less obvious solution to the problem. Thank you for your replies!

entry.title: SAP EWM -- Storage type search with wildcard: No entry found for item 10 for first time putaway
entry.content:
Storage type search with wildcard: No entry found for item 10

Message No. /SCWM/PUT_BIN_DET039

Diagnosis

No entry was found with a full key search nor with a wildcard:

1=WLEW | 2=PAC1 | 3=1010 | 4= | 5=F1 | 6= | 7= | 8=BP--PED | 9= | 10=

The full key search consists of the following fields:

1. Warehouse No.2. Putaway Control3. Whse Process Type/Group4. Qty Classific.5. Stock Type/Group6. Type7. Usage8. Party Entitled to Dispose9. Hazard Rating 110. Hazard Rating 2

Even after doing all STSS and WPT configuration and mapping unable to solve this error what can I do?

entry.title: How can SAP EWM improve yard visibility without creating extra bins or unnecessary warehouse tasks?
entry.content:
In many warehouse operations, when trucks arrive in the yard, teams often create additional storage types, yard bins, or extra warehouse tasks just to improve visibility.

However, SAP EWM already provides core visibility through:

✔️ Transportation Unit (TU) tracking
✔️ Delivery linkage
✔️ Material-level information
✔️ Door and checkpoint monitoring

This raises an important operational question:

Can yard-level delivery and TU visibility be leveraged more effectively for planning and execution without adding unnecessary process complexity?

Goal:

- Reduce excess warehouse tasks
- Avoid over-engineered yard structures
- Improve operational clarity
- Simplify yard-to-warehouse flow
- Use standard SAP EWM capabilities more strategically

Discussion Point:

Would a visibility-first approach at yard level improve efficiency better than expanding warehouse structures for control?

I’d value insights from SAP EWM professionals, architects, and project experts on balancing visibility with process simplicity.

entry.title: Role Management Strategy in EWM Implementation Project (Build Phase vs Production)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
I am currently working on an EWM implementation project and need some guidance on role management during the build phase.
In the build phase, we are creating separate roles for each module. I have a few questions regarding how to handle these roles across systems:
Should these build-phase roles be transported to the Production system, or should they remain only in DEV and QA systems?
If roles are maintained only in DEV and QA, then during system refresh activities, these roles might get deleted. How is this typically handled?
If roles are moved to Production, it may result in many unassigned roles, increasing clutter. Is this acceptable or is there a better way to manage this?
Could you please suggest the best practice for role management in an implementation project, especially for SAP EWM?
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Physical Floor Reality vs SAP EWM Bin Coordinates — Go-Live Risks Nobody Talks About
entry.content:
After 13 years managing warehouse label deployments across hundreds of distribution facilities for Fortune 500 companies, here’s what actually causes physical layer failures at go-live — and it’s not what most SAP consultants expect.
The real issues I’ve seen on the floor:
Structural conflicts — A pillar, beam, or obstruction exists exactly where SAP has a bin location defined. The bin is in the system but physically unreachable or unlabelable. This has to be caught in a site audit before labels are printed.
Coordinate orientation mismatches — If SAP EWM has bin 02-02-D-01 mapped to the east side of the building but the physical layout starts numbering from the west, every single location in that section is wrong. The system is perfect. The floor is perfect. They just don’t match each other.
Label quality failures — Wrinkles during application, ink registration issues from the printer, oil or dust on rack surfaces causing adhesion failure. A label can look installed and still fall off or fail to scan within days.
AI system considerations — As warehouses move toward AI-driven WMS, label placement precision becomes more critical. The system may expect a label in a specific position for automated scanning. A worker can scan a label whether it’s left, center, or right. An automated scanner may not.
The physical layer has to be audited before a single label is printed — not after.
Has anyone developed a pre-go-live physical audit process that accounts for structural conflicts and coordinate orientation before cutover?

entry.title: How do you handle tickets when no one on the team knows the solution?
entry.content:
Hi all,

Something I've come across in a few projects and curious if others face this too.

When a ticket is raised and the person who originally built or configured
that part of the system is unavailable — on leave, moved to another project,
or no longer with the company — how does your team handle it?

Especially in cases where there's no documentation, no handover, and
essentially no one who knows why something was configured a certain way
or what the right fix is.

What's the usual approach? How long do these typically take to resolve
compared to a normal ticket?

Genuine question — would appreciate honest experiences from anyone
who has dealt with this.

entry.title: Looking to understand operational dependency challenges in SAP environments
entry.content:
I’m researching operational support challenges in SAP environments, especially around incidents that require coordination across multiple teams or systems.

I’d love to understand from real project/support experience:

  • What types of SAP incidents take the longest to diagnose?
  • Which issues usually require multiple teams (EWM/MM/BASIS/MES/integration/etc.)?
  • How do teams currently assess downstream impact before making changes?
  • Are there recurring situations where tribal knowledge becomes critical?
  • Have you experienced production issues caused by transports, integrations, queue failures, or configuration changes that unexpectedly affected other processes?
  • How do you currently document cross-system operational dependencies?
  • What operational visibility do you feel is missing today?

Interested in hearing real examples from support projects, warehouse operations, manufacturing integrations, or AMS environments.

entry.title: scope items missing for EWM best practice for S/4 hana 2025
entry.content: scope items missing for EWM best practice for S/4 hana 2025

entry.title: WT creation with POSC assigned by Synchronous Goods Receipts in EWM (migo-putaway)
entry.content:
Hello all,

Do you know if it's possible to trigger POSC assigned to specific WPT during WT creation from migo? (synchronous goods movements) I've tried it, POSC is assigned to WT (putaway) i even tried different approach to assignee it to WOCR but none of approach worked for me.

The task is created, however POSC is not assigned. We need this for HU printing purposes.

results:

config:
